mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand momentum that feeds directly into corporate revenue expectations. Positive momentum reduces earnings uncertainty and drives the S&P 500 higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS monthly change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp one-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points reflects accelerating labor market repair and consumer income recovery. This re-risking impulse generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months meeting the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous reflation under a zero interest rate policy creates a unique wealth transfer from creditors to equity holders. When the federal funds rate is below 0.25% and consumer prices are rising month-over-month, real debt burdens fall and equity risk premia compress, lifting the S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During joint-condition months, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery periods, month-over-month payroll employment growth indicates closing output gaps and declining corporate default risk. This regime-specific information drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery months, the rank correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month surge in industrial production exceeding 1.0% reflects manufacturing inventory restocking and capacity utilization gains. This cyclical impulse raises near-term earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the trigger are negative on average over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) drive downstream demand for construction materials, durable goods, and consumer credit. This operating leverage translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as earnings estimates revise upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after positive HOUST changes do not outperform zero-change months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 4.0% tightens financial conditions, increases corporate interest expense, and raises the equity discount rate. This mechanism causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, the average 20-day forward return is non-negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% annualized confirms aggregate demand is outpacing potential supply, leading to positive revenue surprises and margin expansion. This macro threshold produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2.0% do not coincide with positive average 60-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05).
